creator: madwebness@protonmail.com
text:
Sad to report, but I applied the patch from OpenBSD - in fact, multiple variants of it, posted here - separately, of course, removed the "gdm" pkg and installed "gdm" from ports with the patched "accountsservice" as a dependency.  It didn't hep.

Now I get "Ooops, something went wrong" and the white screen instead of the blank gdm screen. All GNOME requirements are enabled, including the /proc fs.


Before applying it, it was the same story as for everybody: gdm seems to be loaded, I see the mouse and the standard GDM gray background. But no users or icons to click on.

Basically as of right now, it is impossible to install Gnome an gdm on FreeBSD 13.1
